# keepmycarinbankruptcy.com

> Free guide to keeping your car in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy.

This site explains how vehicles are treated in bankruptcy, including exemptions, reaffirmation agreements, redemption, surrender, and the automatic stay's effect on repossession.

## Pages

- [Home](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/): Overview -- can I keep my car in bankruptcy?
- [Chapter 7 Car](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/chapter-7-car.html): Keeping your car in Chapter 7
- [Chapter 13 Car](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/chapter-13-car.html): Keeping your car in Chapter 13
- [Car Exemptions](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/car-exemptions.html): Vehicle exemptions by state
- [Reaffirmation](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/reaffirmation.html): Car loan reaffirmation agreements
- [Surrender Options](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/surrender-options.html): Options for surrendering your vehicle
- [Behind on Payments](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/behind-on-payments.html): Behind on car payments and bankruptcy
- [Repo and Bankruptcy](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/repo-and-bankruptcy.html): Car repossession and bankruptcy
- [FAQ](https://keepmycarinbankruptcy.com/faq.html): Frequently asked questions

## Key Statutes

- 11 U.S.C. Section 362 -- Automatic stay (stops repossession)
- 11 U.S.C. Section 506(a) -- Secured claims / cramdown valuation
- 11 U.S.C. Section 521(a)(2) -- Statement of intention for secured debts
- 11 U.S.C. Section 524(c) -- Reaffirmation agreements
- 11 U.S.C. Section 722 -- Redemption of personal property

## About

Part of the Bankruptcy Tools Network (BTN), a project of the Open Bankruptcy Project, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit (determination pending). Not legal advice.
